subject: Re: Temporary Housing Budget
body:
can I have a picture and floor plan please


----- Original Message ----
From: John Amerling <john_amerling@earthlink.net>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Cc: Charles Miller <cmi3540012@aol.com>; William Rowles <w_rowles@yahoo.com>; daschoettle@yahoo.com
Sent: Tuesday, May 29, 2007 3:28:41 PM
Subject: Temporary Housing Budget


Last week Doug sent design information on the temporary house to Maguire simultaneously requesting pricing from the vendor to be completed when Doug returned. Dan informed us that you wished to accelerate the pricing effort. We are pleased to do so. Accordingly, Charles, Bill and I reviewed the design information generated by Doug. Bill and I met with the supplier locally and negotiated with the vendor’s home office on Friday and over the weekend. Based upon independent estimates performed by Bill and Charles we are submitting the following budget price for the layout of the four units shown on the rough drawing prepared by Doug which is attached to this note:
 
Base Budget Price: $882,338, delivered, erected and finished by Vendor with assistance from Lafayette at the site as follows: 
Includes: 
All structural engineering (by Vendor) 
All site work and slab 
All utilities and wastewater treatment at the site 
Complete Shell with connecting porches including, for all materials and tool: all transportation, clearing, customs, and taxes to LSJ 
Allowance for exterior Doors and Windows of $29,000 
Allowance for electrical, mechanical and finishes of $421,600 
Excludes: 
Transportation of vendor personnel between St. Thomas and LSJ during erection 
Utilities to site, i.e. from RO/Generator to Flagpole area (now being priced separately as part of the overall infrastructure program) 
Cost of use and operation of LSJ equipment for excavation and lifting of components 
Landscaping 
Hardscaping 
Items which could be selected and added to the price 
Cistern $47,850 
Contingency of 10% or $88,234 
 
This budget is based upon our understanding of information provided by Doug, the scope of which may change in further discussions which you may have with him when he returns
 
 
Lafayette Contractors LLC
6100 Red Hook Quarter, Suite A2-7
St. Thomas, VI 00802
Tel: 340 779-8054
Fax:340 779-8055
Cell:340 690-7194

can I have a picture and floor plan please

----- Original Message ----
From: John Amerling <john_amerling@earthlink.net>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Cc: Charles Miller <cmi3540012@aol.com>; William Rowles <w_rowles@yahoo.com>; daschoettle@yahoo.com
Sent: Tuesday, May 29, 2007 3:28:41 PM
Subject: Temporary Housing Budget

Last week Doug sent design information on the temporary house to Maguire simultaneously requesting pricing from the vendor to be completed when Doug returned. Dan informed us that you wished to accelerate the pricing effort. We are pleased to do so. Accordingly, Charles, Bill and I reviewed the design information generated by Doug. Bill and I met with the supplier locally and negotiated with the vendor’s home office on Friday and over the weekend. Based upon independent estimates performed by Bill and Charles we are submitting the following budget price for the layout of the four units shown on the rough drawing prepared by Doug which is attached to this note:

 

  • Base Budget Price: $882,338, delivered, erected and finished by Vendor with assistance from Lafayette at the site as follows:
  • Includes:
    • All structural engineering (by Vendor)
    • All site work and slab
    • All utilities and wastewater treatment at the site
    • Complete Shell with connecting porches including, for all materials and tool: all transportation, clearing, customs, and taxes to LSJ
    • Allowance for exterior Doors and Windows of $29,000
    • Allowance for electrical, mechanical and finishes of $421,600
  • Excludes:
    • Transportation of vendor personnel between St. Thomas and LSJ during erection
    • Utilities to site, i.e. from RO/Generator to Flagpole area (now being priced separately as part of the overall infrastructure program)
    • Cost of use and operation of LSJ equipment for excavation and lifting of components
    • Landscaping
    • Hardscaping
  • Items which could be selected and added to the price
    • Cistern $47,850
    • Contingency of 10% or $88,234

 

This budget is based upon our understanding of information provided by Doug, the scope of which may change in further discussions which you may have with him when he returns

 

 

Lafayette Contractors LLC

6100 Red Hook Quarter, Suite A2-7

St. Thomas, VI 00802
